title: Multi-repo driver: auto-discover, auto-clone, and sweep all adw backlogs until quota (sequential v1)
description:
## Problem

Cross-repo work has no unattended path. `ADW_REPO` selects one target per
process; an interactive `$env:ADW_REPO` never reaches the Task Scheduler job
(it's a separate process tree reading the persistent environment, where the var
is empty by design); so the overnight automation only ever works the engine
itself. Per-repo scheduled tasks + env vars don't scale. The repo-agnostic
engine (S-011) was step one toward multi-repo; this is the driver layer on top.

Note: syncing/retrieving issues is already deterministic Python (no LLM, no
tokens) — `workflows/sync_issues.py` (REST + regex). Token cost is only the
work stages (plan/implement/test/review). So sweeping every repo's issues is
free; the only bounded resource is the work, which the circuit breaker already
governs. Hence: no artificial ticket cap — work until quota'd or backlogs empty.

## Decisions (locked)

- **Discovery:** auto-discover all the account's GitHub repos that have ≥1 open
  `adw`-labeled issue (no maintained list).
- **Local clones:** auto-clone any missing repo into a managed dir.
- **No global cap:** sweep and work as much as possible until quota'd or empty.
- **Shared breaker/quota:** ONE account-wide breaker + cooldown across all repos.
- **Ordering:** round-robin one ticket per repo (so a quota wall mid-sweep leaves
  some progress everywhere rather than starving later repos).
- **Sequential v1** — parallel deferred until the #4 worktree work lands.

## Approach

A new driver (e.g. `workflows/poll_all.py`) that:
1. Discovers targets: list the account's repos via the API, keep those with an
   open `adw` issue.
2. Ensures local clones: clone missing repos into a managed dir; resolve each
   repo's working-tree path (idempotent; `git fetch` if already present).
3. Syncs every repo (reuse `sync_issues`), populating each repo's own `prd.json`.
4. Works tickets round-robin across repos until the shared breaker trips (quota)
   or all backlogs are empty — reusing `run_one_story`/`run_ticket` per ticket,
   re-resolving `paths.target_root()` per repo (clear its `lru_cache` between
   repos).
5. On quota: the current repo's ticket goes `quotad`; the driver writes the
   GLOBAL cooldown and stops the whole sweep.
6. Resumes on the next scheduled firing once the global cooldown elapses
   (`quotad` + open tickets are picked up automatically).

## Key new pieces

- Repo **discovery** (API: list repos → filter to those with an open `adw` issue).
- **Auto-clone manager** (managed dir, idempotent).
- **Driver loop** with per-repo `target_root` cache reset + per-repo failure
  isolation (one repo's clone/sync/ticket error never aborts the sweep).
- **Lift the breaker cooldown to a global, out-of-repo store** (e.g.
  `%LOCALAPPDATA%/adw/breaker.json`) — checked before the sweep, written on a
  quota halt. This is the core change that makes the breaker account-wide instead
  of per-`state.json`.
- **Round-robin** cross-repo ticket selection.

## Reuses unchanged

`sync_issues`, `run_one_story`/`run_ticket`, the `quotad` path
(`_finalize_story`: no PR, observer skipped, cooldown from parsed reset time),
the GH-47 stale-`in_progress` reaper, the autocommit/guard/stop hooks, per-repo
`prd.json`/`state.json`.

## Deferred / out of scope

- Parallel cross-repo execution (needs the unmerged #4 worktrees).
- GitHub Project (v2) as a discovery source (GraphQL; the label scan covers v1).
- Wake-at-exact-reset precision (hourly re-fire + cooldown gate suffices).
acceptance_criteria: One invocation discovers all repos with an open `adw` issue, auto-clones any
The circuit breaker / cooldown is account-wide: a quota halt in ANY repo writes
Per-repo failure isolation: a clone error, sync error, or bad ticket in one
No artificial ticket cap — the breaker is the only stop (besides empty
Self-host (no other repos with `adw` issues) behaves like today.
Unit tests with a fake API/invoke cover: discovery + filter, round-robin across
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
Auto-discover means any repo ever labeled `adw` joins the sweep (intentional).
Auto-clone writes to disk / pulls over the network.
A no-cap sweep can run for hours (until the ~5h session limit) — fine
The account-wide quota is the real ceiling; the global breaker must trip
